package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "math/rand"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for rand_new_per_call: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `rand.New(...)` inside regular call paths instead of reusing a generator.
type CaseRandNewPerCallDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseRandNewPerCallModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseRandNewPerCallRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseRandNewPerCallModel
}

type CaseRandNewPerCallService struct {
    repo *CaseRandNewPerCallRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseRandNewPerCallAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveRandNewPerCall(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseRandNewPerCallDTO) error {
    focus := "rand_new_per_call"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    _ = rand.New(rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano())).Intn(10)
    for _, item := range items { _ = strings.ToLower(item.ID); time.Now() }
    rand_new_per_call_positive := map[string]int{"rand": 1, "new": 2, "per": 3, "call": 4}
    rand_new_per_call_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    rand_positive_0 := rand_new_per_call_positive["rand"] + len(input)
    _ = rand_positive_0
    new_positive_1 := rand_new_per_call_positive["new"] + len(input)
    _ = new_positive_1
    for per_positive_2Index, item := range items { rand_new_per_call_positive[item.ID] = per_positive_2Index }
    call_positive_3 := rand_new_per_call_positive["call"] + len(input)
    _ = call_positive_3
    return nil
}
